title: Sasaella glabra
taxonomy: Sasaella glabra is classified as a species of bamboo in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Sasaella, which is a type of woody bamboo. Sasaella glabra is native to East Asia and is characterized by its smooth and glabrous stems. This species is often found in shaded or woodland areas and is known for its ability to form dense stands. Sasaella glabra is a perennial plant that grows through underground rhizomes, allowing it to spread quickly in suitable environments.
